feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Concur
feed.subtitle: SAP Concur blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: Automating Tax Reclamation with SAP Concur API
entry.content:
1. Introduction

This document outlines the use case for automating tax reclamation submissions using the SAP Concur API. By leveraging the /api/v3.0/taxes/reclamation endpoint with a POST request, organizations can significantly reduce administrative burden, accelerate tax refunds, and streamline processes in regions with complex tax reclaim procedures.

  1. Use Case

Many countries allow businesses to reclaim certain taxes, such as Value Added Tax (VAT) or Goods and Services Tax (GST), on eligible expenses. However, the process of submitting these claims can be time-consuming and error-prone when done manually. The SAP Concur API provides a solution to automate this process, offering several benefits:

  • Reduced administrative workload
  • Faster processing of tax refunds
  • Improved accuracy in submissions
  • Better compliance with local tax regulations
  • Real-time tracking of reclamation status
  1. Country-Specific Examples

3.1 United Kingdom (UK)

In the UK, businesses can reclaim VAT on many goods and services purchased for business use. The SAP Concur API can be used to automatically submit VAT reclamation forms to HM Revenue and Customs (HMRC).

3.2 Australia

Australian businesses can claim GST credits for eligible business purchases. The SAP Concur API can streamline the process of submitting Business Activity Statements (BAS) to the Australian Taxation Office (ATO).

  1. Implementation

To implement the tax reclamation automation, you'll need to make a POST request to the /api/v3.0/taxes/reclamation endpoint. Here's a sample code snippet in Python using the requests library:

import requests
import json

# Replace with your SAP Concur API credentials
api_url = "https://api.concursolutions.com/api/v3.0/taxes/reclamation"
access_token = "YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN"

# Sample data for UK VAT reclamation
uk_reclamation_data = {
    "country": "GB",
    "reclamationType": "VAT",
    "claimPeriodStart": "2023-01-01",
    "claimPeriodEnd": "2023-03-31",
    "totalReclaimAmount": 1000.00,
    "currency": "GBP",
    "expenses": [
        {
            "expenseId": "EXP12345",
            "reclaimAmount": 200.00,
            "taxRate": 20
        },
        # Add more expenses as needed
    ]
}

# Sample data for Australian GST reclamation
au_reclamation_data = {
    "country": "AU",
    "reclamationType": "GST",
    "claimPeriodStart": "2023-04-01",
    "claimPeriodEnd": "2023-06-30",
    "totalReclaimAmount": 1500.00,
    "currency": "AUD",
    "expenses": [
        {
            "expenseId": "EXP67890",
            "reclaimAmount": 100.00,
            "taxRate": 10
        },
        # Add more expenses as needed
    ]
}

headers = {
    "Authorization": f"Bearer {access_token}",
    "Content-Type": "application/json"
}

# Submit UK VAT reclamation
uk_response = requests.post(api_url, headers=headers, data=json.dumps(uk_reclamation_data))
print(f"UK VAT Reclamation Status: {uk_response.status_code}")
print(f"UK VAT Reclamation Response: {uk_response.json()}")

# Submit Australian GST reclamation
au_response = requests.post(api_url, headers=headers, data=json.dumps(au_reclamation_data))
print(f"Australian GST Reclamation Status: {au_response.status_code}")
print(f"Australian GST Reclamation Response: {au_response.json()}")
  • Conclusion

By leveraging the SAP Concur API for tax reclamation, organizations can significantly improve their financial processes, ensure compliance, and accelerate tax refunds. This automation is particularly valuable for businesses operating in countries with complex tax reclaim procedures, such as the UK and Australia.

For more information on implementing this solution or for assistance with other SAP Concur API integrations, please refer to the official SAP Concur API documentation or contact SAP support.

entry.title: I am a business user where can I start learning the basics of SAP Concur request?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Getting Started with Concur Professional Request for Business Users learning journey.


Overview
This learning journey is an introduction to Concur Request for Business Users. In this comprehensive journey, you will learn the basics of Concur Request, from creating and working with requests to reviewing and approving them as an approver.

Learning objectives
After completing this course, business users will be able to proficiently manage their requests by using the Concur request platform to submit, approve, and process requests. 

Prerequisites
A general understanding of SAP Concur including Concur Expense and Concur Travel. 

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.
Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: SAP Concur Native Integration – Replicating and Posting with Real and Statistical Cost Objects
entry.content:
Dealing with Statistical Cost Objects on SAP side might seem to add a bit to the difficulty of setting up and integrating Concur with an SAP ERP, but I always make sure I understand exactly the “use case”, as sometimes everything will be easier than it seems. Normally having a statistical cost object means that in certain situations a Concur Claim / Report or even a specific entry needs to be posted against multiple cost objects in the same time (usually a “real” cost object for accounting and a “statistical” one for reporting purposes). In Concur, the only way to allow this is to have these “real” and “statistical” objects maintained in separate lists, as a single list can only provide one selection option at one time.

Before anything else and to determine the correct “use case” always consider the following scenarios and implications / limitations, from easiest to the most challenging:

  1. Is there a 1:1 relation maintained in SAP between the “real” and “statistical”? SAP can automatically derive one of the needed cost objects during posting if the other one is provided. For example, if for one or multiple Cost Centers there is always the same Project / WBS element assign then maintaining and allocating only against the Cost Centers in Concur will be enough to ensure the derivation of the Project / WBS element in SAP FI during posting. This would be the easiest scenario as we will not even have to replicate or maintain the second Cost Object Type in Concur (in this case, we consider Cost Centers as needed in our Concur Hierarchy as they are mandatory for the Employee profile, so we replicate and maintain Cost Centers in our main Company Hierarchy but disregard the Project / WBS elements as they will be derived automatically during posting).
  2. Are the used cost object types always “real” or “statistical”, or can some types be both? For example, if we have real cost centers and statistical internal orders, are all internal orders always “statistical”, or do we have a combination of real and statistical internal orders? There are several implications here:
  • Since in Concur we always need to keep them in separate lists to allow the option of multiple selection we also need to understand if a cost object type (CC, IO, WBS etc) is expected to always be in the same list or in both
  • In SAP we are also limited at one replication wizard per each supported cost object type, so if the cost objects are always the same then we can easily point the different replication wizards to different lists in Concur. For example we can replicate all Cost Centers to our Concur Company Hierarchy list, while all Internal Orders can be replicated in our Statistical list.

This scenario, while a bit more difficult than the first one is still fairly easy, as it allows us to replicate all needed cost objects in different lists and to map them accordingly for Financial Posting, with no need for BAdIs on either replication or posting.

  • The most challenging scenario is when none of the above applies, and we are dealing with cost object types containing both “real” and “statistical” objects, which mean that while we need both in Concur, in SAP via ICS we can treat either one or the other.

Here is a step by step example on the easiest and most typical way to address this scenario:

 

Cost object replication:  

 

  1. SAP ICS should be used to replicate your real cost objects (i.e, Cost Centers, WBS elements, Internal Orders) from your SAP system into Concur.  You can do that by using the relevant cost object wizard available with ICS in your SAP system.

 

 

  1.  A change to the BAdI associated with each cost object type that you replicate should be implemented in order to excludes statistical cost objects from the data being extracted by these wizards.  Each of the master data tables that hold their associated cost object information has a field to identify a cost object as statistical.   This field can be used for filtering purposes.

 

Cost Center:

WBS Elements:

Orders:

 

  1.  In Concur there should be a separate list within Concur to hold a complete list of statistical cost objects.   While a single level list could be used, we recommend creating a two-level list where the first level is a cost object type and the second is the cost object code itself in the event that you have multiple statistical cost object types.

 

 

  1.  In Concur we should add this new two-level list to the allocation form of the expense report.   Note that both real and statistical Cost Objects will now be available for selection. By design the statistical cost objects are not being setup as required fields.

 

  1.  A separate list import file should be created on the SAP FI side to extract the statistical cost objects and populate them into the new list that was created for them. Below is a screen shot of what the list import file would look like.

  1.  Now the expense user creating an expense report should be able to select both real and statistical cost objects at the allocation level of their expense report.  

  1.  The Financial Posting wizard of SAP ICS allows mapping the fields from the allocation level of the expense report and this mapping should be used for the real cost objects associated with the posting. The wizard will by default only allow mapping based on the cost object replication setups, and normally we only map the allocation related to the main Company Hierarchy list. 

 

 

  1.  SAP ICS does not by default support the use of statistical cost objects.   As a result, a BAdI change will be needed to pick up the statistical cost objects placed into the separate list from the JSON file produced from the expense report and map them into the financial posting as statistical.

 I hope the above sheds a bit more light on how to deal with statistical cost object from an Integration perspective, and what are the various implications and limitations that should be considered.

Thank you, and I wish everyone only successful integrations!

Alex

entry.title: A Step by Step Guide for ICS Integration.
entry.content:
Hi Everyone, I know this is very basic and most of you all would be aware about the process but still when I tried to do for my client I did hit a few roadblocks, so I thought why not make it easy for someone new and doing this for the first time.

So first what is ICS? Basically ICS integration with SAP Concur gives a seamless data exchange between SAP'S ERP systems and Concur. Once this integration is enabled it becomes very easy and straightforward to manage expense reports posting to SAP along with master data, company's org structure details like company code and cost center etc. We all have had our troubles with reconciling the SAE extracts if they fail to post right? I bet you you have because it is such a manual process to manually correct the details and send the file again, but with SAP ICS enabled everything is just a click away.

End of the day SAP ICS integration with Concur creates a more efficient, accurate and user-friendly expense management process for organizations using SAP solutions.

Now coming to how to get this integration done with Concur and SAP's ERP solution that we are using?

Lets first see what all are required from our end. Assuming everyone to be client partners working on implementation or supporting the clients for AMS work, we should have unrestricted access to the Concur system.

Once we have that, lets go through the step by step process to activate this integration.

Step 1:

Go to Home->Administration->Authentication Admin

This will take you to the Authentication Admin page where you will see 2 options.

  1. Company Request Token

     2.OAuth 2.0 Application Management

We will first click on OAuth 2.0 Application Management.

OAuth 2.0 is a framework which enables applications to access resources with each other in a secure way i.e we don't need to give away our username and password to every application we connect Concur with instead we can enable certain permissions to access only the data each system needs.

So this page lets us to create an application to which we are going to connect Concur with by providing details like App name, App Type, App description.

Then comes the part where we allow certain Grants/Scopes like password, client credentials, refresh token etc.

And after this is where we can allow what all data can be accessed in the Allowed Scopes section. You can get the complete list of scopes from this very clear link SAP Concur Developer Center | Learn More About Scopes.

Then there is a check box which we need to check if we are planning to create an SAP ICS Application. Make sure you check that.

Once we have completed this step and click on Submit, we will be given the Client ID , Client Secret and Company UUID.

These three strings are important which will be used later but for now we just need to copy them and keep it in a word document or a notepad.

Step 2: 

Click on Company Request Token.

So this Request token is nothing but a key which is issued by Concur which needs to be given to the external application when it wants to connect with Concur using OAuth 2.0.

To generate a Request token, click on Company Request Token and enter the client id which you had saved from the previous step in the App name field and click on submit. Please note that each Request token generated will last only for 24hrs. If the connection is not established by then we have to request for a new Request token.

Now the Concur part is completed and we need to work on the SAP part.

Step 3:

Log in to the SAP application and use the T-Code CTE_SETUP

Under CTE_SETUP, Click on System->Create Connection

After this there is a step by step process where we need to give few details as seen below, (since it contains client information I have masked out the details) and click on next

Now comes the part where we need to enter the information we obtained from Concur which we saved in a notepad. And select the use Request Token option and give continue and verify the details in the next page and then complete the process.

When we complete following things happen.

  • The system generates the RFC connection.
  • The credentials are saved to the secure store and are required to refresh the access token.

Step 4:

Check Connection.

Now that we have established the connection it is time to check if the connection is complete.

For this use the below navigation.

T-Code CTE_SETUP > System > Check Connection

You should see a screen as below.

Step 5:

Set up the the cost object structure as per our Concur list management for Cost center, WBS and Internal Order.

 

 

Step 6 :

Setting up the Financial posting import.

Now in the next page select document type TE and check IBCP credit card to enable credit card payments.

Provide the other details like cost object mapping and keep clicking on next. Now when you are in the Attachments screen, Select “All documents of expense report”. In Store attachments using Select “ArchiveLink” >  “content Repository = AM Test Invoices in nscale” and add manually “Global Document Type = CTE_DOCS”.

In the Automatic Import Section, choose the type you want whether it is manual or automatic and Job Name CTE_FIN_FEEDBACK_LOOP 

Before clicking on complete validate the details we had given in each field and make sure they are correct.

Once you click on complete ICS connection is established between Concur and SAP.

Step 7:

While creating group configurations for new countries make sure to check the financial 

So there it is a step by step guide to make this integration an easier process for any newcomer with required details. I wanted to do this because I did not get something like this online so anyone who find themselves here going through these steps in the future I hope it did help you or at least gave a good idea on how to enable ICS integration.

Thanks a lot Everyone!

entry.title: How to get Audit Trail Section in Print Reports in SAP Concur
entry.content:
I always look up the internet for ideas and solutions whenever I get stuck with something and finding what we are looking for can become a tedious task especially in niche technologies like Concur. And when I don't find the exact thing but later come up with a solution, I tend to make a playbook so that it might come in handy in future. But now that I have got an idea how blogging works, I feel this is even better as it reaches a wider audience and can help someone like me who is finding a solution.

So coming to the task that made me sweep the various documents and posts was a very valid ask from one of our business team members. They wanted to have the Audit Trail section come in the Print Report so that they can download it.

The business case as I understood from him was many times the admin team members who do the initial audit get blamed for Late fees, which happens when the report is not processed on time.

The Audit team find it difficult to get into the audit trail section for those reports which are in question take a screenshot of the day and time they have approved it from their to ensure it was not them who were the reason for late fees but the submission from users were found to be delayed.

This cumbersome manual process is something we can eliminate if we can have a Print Report which gives out the Audit Trail section. They can just download the PDF and send it to the respective stakeholders.

So when trying to check out the existing templates we do not have any with regards to Audit trail. And when you go to the internet and browse there is just one similar question back from 2022 which was never answered.

And then when you check the training documents from concur only the below portion is mentioned 

  • Audit Trail: Displays audit trail data, including confirmation agreement acceptance records, to the printed report. This data appears in a non-configurable preset format.

Displays audit trail data, including confirmation agreement acceptance records, to the printed report. This data appears in a non-configurable preset format.

Syntax: $auditdata_XXXX

(where XXXX is the content section name, such as $auditdata_AuditTrail)

Also to note is The printed report will create the audit trail information based on the role of the user who is printing the report. For example, a processor sees more types of information on the audit trail than a submitter. When the processor prints the report, their audit trail section will contain more details than when the submitter prints the same report.

But apart from that there is no real information about anything. So I have come up with the HTML Code that will ease up the process and also create a template which will include only the Audit Trail section.

The Actual Code:

<tr>
<td>
<table class="cStdForm"
cellspacing="0"
style="width: 100%"
cellpadding="0"
border="0"
summary="$!ui.localize('table.summary.printreportreceipt')">
<tr><td> $auditdata_AuditTrailSection </td></tr>
</table>
</td>
</tr>

Now that we have the code it is just a matter of fitting it into a template and assigning that Print report to the required policy.

Step 1:

Go to Home->Expense->Printed Reports->Print Templates

Here copy an existing template and rename it . Then open the newly copied report and in the template code make sure to place our code which is given above in a place where you desire. The position can be seen by clicking on the preview layout. Then click on save.

Make sure you are careful with all the open and close tags when you remove contents you don't need from the template. 

Step 2:

Now move to the Print Formats tab. Similar way copy an existing Format and rename it. In the Print 

template field choose the template we just created in the previous step.

and preview the layout and make sure it is as per the requirements.

Now go back to the Print Format tab and click on Modify content

In the Modify Content page you will see all the headers of the sections we have inserted in the template.

Now click on the Audit Trail and click on Modify content.

In the modify content of the Audit trail section give a suitable header and also make sure you check the Print Confirmation Agreement Audit trail records check box.

Step 3:

Now head to the policy where this Print Report is required and enable it at Policy level.

Once this is done the go to the processor, search for the report for which we need the Audit Trail to be printed out and download the same.

The final output of the PDF look like below with the Employee data and the Audit trail section.

So there it is , a way to add the Audit Trail section to the Print Template and I hope this will help anyone who are looking for a solution to this type of a requirement.

Thanks a lot everyone!

entry.title: Expert Feedback: Options for changing a credit card liability type within Concur
entry.content:
Hi all, as a Technical Consultant at Concur I setup a lot of credit card feeds for our customers.  One common question that I get is related to updating the liability type on an existing credit card feed.   Specifically, our customers want to know whether they need to purchase a new feed with Concur or whether we can make changes to the existing one they already have.  This can be a complex issue and there are several things to consider.   In this blog post, I will attempt to address this question using the most common scenarios that we see.   

Ok…before we get too deep into the topic, I want to start by defining the different liability types that are available.  Hopefully these will be familiar to you.   I have seen occasions where a customer may use a slightly different name for a liability type, but in the end they always align to one of these.

  • IBIP (Individual Bill Individual Pay) – This type of credit card is very much like an employee’s personal card.  These cards are the employee’s personal liability, and they are responsible for making payments to them whenever a bill comes due.   When this type of card is used in a business environment, it is the employee’s responsibility to expense any business transactions that they made so they can be reimbursed the funds they need to pay the card for those expenses.   Within Concur, a credit card that is setup with an IBIP liability type will trigger a cash reimbursement payable to the employee.
  • IBCP (Individual Bill Company Pay) -  This type of credit card has a shared liability with the company.   The employee will receive the statements for the credit card, but the expectation is that the company will be making payments on the employee’s behalf based on submitted expense reports.   If the employee does not submit expense reports for this type of card, the account will go delinquent, and both the employee and company can be held liable.  
  • CBCP (Company Bill Company Pay) – This type of card is the liability of the company.   The company is responsible for making payments for this type of card monthly.   The company normally makes their payment from a billing statement that is produced by the credit card vendor.   The company will make this payment regardless of whether the employee has submitted expense reports detailing the types of expenses that were incurred.  Most of the time, a company will complete a reconciliation between the amounts paid to the credit card vendor and the amounts submitted on expense reports to ensure that un-expensed transactions do not grow over time. 

Now that we are aligned on the different liability types available, I’ll get back to the original question about changing the liability type of an existing feed.  Here are the four most common scenarios that we see when making a liability type change.  These scenarios apply to all of the liability types mentioned above and the process does not differ based on liability type.

  • The credit card vendor plans to issue new cards to card holders using a new feed.
  • The credit card vendor plans to issue new cards to card holders using the existing feed.
  • The credit card vendor plans to move existing cards into a new feed with Concur.
  • The credit card vendor plans to use the existing cards in the existing feed. 

I have placed these options in order from most desirable to least desirable in terms of following our best practices.   Read on below as I go through each one.

Option 1: The credit card vendor issues new cards to card holders using a new feed.

From a Concur perspective, this is really the best-case scenario.   The issuing of new card accounts opens the door for the assignment of a new payment type in Concur.   The payment type controls the liability type used on the card and hence determines Concur’s processing method for transactions on that given card.

This is the cleanest approach because it allows for a clean cutover between the old liability type and the new one.   All activity under the old liability type would continue to flow through Concur as it normally would and the new transactions with the new liability type will come in on the new feed just like those from a completely different credit card vendor.  Transactions for both the old and new liability type can exist within Concur at the same time without any issue and there is no cleanup or blackout period required.  

This approach is also the cleanest from a reporting standpoint.   Since the payment type name is created and a new feed was added to Concur, you have two additional data points that can be used to separate activity being processed under the old liability type vs the new one.  Yes, there is a relatively small fee associated with the setup of the new feed, but as you read through these options you will see that in most cases this fee is offset by a reduction in overall effort required for the transition.

Option 2:  The credit card vendor issues new cards to card holders using the existing feed.

Like with option 1, the assignment of new card accounts provides an opportunity for Concur to assign a new payment type for the new cards.   However, since the existing feed is going to be re-used a short blackout period is needed to ensure that all transactions made under the old liability type are processed through to Concur before the payment type can be updated on the credit card feed. 

Concur will need to stop the credit card feed and receive confirmation from the bank regarding the name of the first file that contains transactions with the new liability type.   This is critical because the payment type that is setup with the import job needs to be updated, but it can not be updated until the new file is received.   From a processing standpoint, any transaction that refers to the old payment type will continue to carry the old liability type and the new cards received in the first new file will contain the new payment type and its associated new liability type.  

Transactions can be processed within the same expense report because the assignment of a new payment type ensures that Concur processes the new transactions differently from the old ones.   From a reporting standpoint, the new payment type name can be used to distinguish the old liability type from the new one.      

Option 3:  The credit card vendor moves existing cards into a new feed with Concur.

This is really only a viable option if the number of active cards and outstanding credit card transactions in the system is relatively low.   Let me explain. 

Once a transaction has been imported into Concur and added to an expense report that transaction will retain its assigned liability type even if the associated payment type is changed after the fact.   As a result, all of the transactions currently loaded into the system must be pushed through to posting before any new transactions that should carry the new liability type are imported.      

This requirement usually results in a blackout period where the feed is stopped and all new files that should be processed with the new liability type are held back from being imported while the customer works on the cleanup of the old activity.   Care should be taken to ensure that all transactions are cleaned up in the site and the assistance of someone from Concur may be needed to ensure that all transactions are accounted for. 

Once the cleanup is complete, the customer has two options. 

  1. Re-use the existing payment type and just update the liability type.
  2. Create a new payment type with the new liability type to use with the new feed.

Re-using the existing payment type is possible, but is not recommended because it makes it more difficult to identify transactions that were processed with the old liability type vs the new one.  Since a new feed is also being setup under this option, it is possible to incorporate the old or new feed into any reporting that is being done, but the payment type itself is a much more recognizable and widely understood data point than the credit card import feed.

Creating a new payment type makes the reporting piece easier as discussed above but it does come with a price.  Once a card is assigned to a payment type, the only way that it can be changed is through the UI in Concur.   It cannot be updated through an interface.   This means that even if the payment type was updated on the credit card feed, it will not update the existing accounts that have already been assigned a payment type.  This associated manual update is why we would only consider this type of conversion for customers that have less than 1500 credit cards in use.

Option 4: The credit card vendor uses the existing cards in the existing feed. 

This is the least preferred option and the most difficult to carry out.   It is very similar to option three and carries all of the restrictions and downsides mentioned in option three.  Specifically, it should only be considered if the number of cards and outstanding transactions is relatively low.  A blackout and cleanup period will be required and you will lose all ability to accurately report on transactions being processed under the old liability type vs the new liability type unless you go through with making the manual updates associated with setting up a new payment type mentioned above.    

Wrap up:

Ok…so that is it.   I hope you found this blog post useful.   There are some small variations possible on each of the scenarios mentioned above, but I have tried stick with best practices and feel confident that these cover the primary scenarios you will face.  Please note that if you have an open project with Concur, your assigned Project Manager and Technical Consultant can walk you through each step in the process.   We know that there is already a lot of change required on your side when it comes to updating the credit card liability type and we want to help make the transition as easy as possible for you.

entry.title: Where can I learn how to configure the SAP Concur application to protect data?
entry.content:
I  recommend to access our Enhancing Security in Your SAP Concur Environment learning journey.

Overview
This course aids those in Customer Authorized Support Contact (ASC) roles who administer SAP Concur, who play a crucial role in maintaining security within their organization. You, as our valued customer, are empowered to configure the SAP Concur application in a way that benefits your organization and protects your data.

Learning objectives
In this course, as authorized support contacts, you will be able to implement strong password policies, conduct regular security audits, and ensure your environment is secure and well configured, using the Collaborative Approach Model in your SAP Concur environment.

Prerequisites
  • Must have the Authorized Support Contact (ASC) permission assigned.

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 
I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Getting Employees into Concur: Employee Import Options
entry.content:
USER ADMINISTRATION

As an administrator myself, I used this tool daily. I was responsible for troubleshooting any employee import errors or warnings, making unscheduled updates to user profiles, and managing delegate assignments. Even if your primary method of adding and maintaining users will be using the flat file employee import or web services, it is still a very useful tool for administrators and highly recommended.

PERMISSION: User Administration

Administrative permissions are required to access User Administration within a Concur site. This permission is User Administration. (You may also find this named Employee Administration.)
With this role, an administrator may:

  • add users
  • view existing users
  • edit user profiles, including assigning user and approver roles

This role may be group aware—which means a user may only be allowed to use this permission for the group assigned.

WHY USE THIS METHOD?
This method is very helpful for administrators to make quick changes outside of any normally scheduled processes. Administrators can also use this method to validate data from a flat file or web service update. Additional functions may be available based on your site’s specific configuration, such as delegate management.

RESOURCES:
User Administration User Guide

USER IMPORT

The User Import is a function within User Administration that allows you to download a template to add, edit, and disable users.

There are three templates to choose from:

  • User Import Information
    • Use this to create a user profile and assign Expense, Invoice, and Request user and approver roles.
    • You can also assign credit cards to users and card programs (if applicable).
  • Credit Card Information
    • Use this to create credit card accounts, assign them to cardholders, and assign them to card programs (if applicable).
  • Bank Account Information
    • If using employee banking, this import allows you to import employee banking information.

PERMISSION: User Administration

WHY USE THIS METHOD?
Customers who do not plan to build a flat file employee import file with the intent to regularly send an employee import file (generally via sFTP) may choose to use this method. This can especially be used to initially load users for customers who plan to maintain user profiles manually.

RESOURCES:
User Import User Guide

FLAT FILE

The employee import flat file will add, edit, and terminate users. There are several employee import records, each with its own purpose and task(s).

There are two ways to import the employee import file. (Resources provided below.)

  1. HTTPS Upload/Download - You must have the Import/Extract Monitor permission to manually upload a file.
  1. sFTP - An sFTP account must be configured with SSH key and the file must be encrypted with Concur’s PGP key.

EMPLOYEE IMPORT RECORDS
There are 27 employee import records, but you will not use all of these in your site. These are the primary records most customers use:

  • 100 record – Required
    • The 100 record contains the import settings and is required for all employee files imported. In this record, you are telling Concur what you want it to do with the data on the following records.
  • 305 Enhanced Employee
    • This record builds the foundation of the employee profile. In this record, you will assign the policy, groups, and default budgets for each user. You will also assign user and approver permissions for the various Concur modules you may be using (Travel, Expense, Request, Invoice).
    • *If your company only has Concur Travel, you may use the 310 record to build the foundation profile.
  • 350 Travel Addendum
    • If your company has Travel & Expense, you may choose to include the 350 record to add traveler details like contact information and traveler details. This is not a required record but can be helpful to build out a traveler’s profile. If you have more than one travel rule class configured, you can use this record to assign the correct travel rule class to each user.

Depending on your configuration, you may need to use other employee import records. Some of these may be a one-time-only feed at the beginning of your implementation, or you may choose to send them regularly for maintenance.

  • 370 Employee Purchasing Card and 650 Enhanced Card Account
    • If you are using Company Billed Statements (CBS), you will want to use the 370 record to assign the CBS user and approver permissions as well as assign the statement approver for each cardholder. The 650 record is used to load your p-cards, assign them to the cardholders, and also assign them to the CBS program configured in your site.
  • 700 series records for Authorized Approver and Cost Object Approver add and remove

There are five records in the 700 series—each with their own task. Depending on how your workflows are configured for Expense, Request, and/or Invoice, you may need to use these records to assign Authorized Approvers and/or Cost Object Approvers to budgets.

  • 700 (Authorized Approver Importer) – Add an Authorized Approver with or without limits
  • 710 (Cost Object Approver Importer) – Add a Cost Object Approver
  • 720 (Authorized Approver With Level Importer) – Add an Authorized Approver with level assignment
  • 750 (Delete Authorized Approver Importer) – Remove any Authorized Approver
  • 760 (Delete Cost Object Approver Importer) – Remove any Cost Object Approver

The specific file you need will depend on your configuration.

PERMISSION: Import/Extract Monitor
Whether you are adding users for Travel*, Expense, Request, or Invoice, you will need the Import/Extract Monitor permission assigned to your profile. This permission provides access to Import/Extract Administrator (Administration > Tools > Import/Extract Administrator) where you may:

  • View the run status for each import and extract job
  • Import files manually
  • Extract files manually
  • Manually run import and/or extract jobs (if permission is enabled in your site)

This role is recommended for anyone who is administering your Concur site. You may consider also adding this permission to someone in IT who oversees the file management process and someone in Human Resources who can help with data validation since the information in the employee import records typically comes from an HR system.

Reviewing the import status is an important task for your Concur Administrator.

*Travel must be connected to an Expense site to use an employee import flat file.

WHY USE THIS METHOD?
Customers who have several employees and want their employee profiles to be updated daily will use flat files to manage their Concur user data. Building a program to extract the data from their human capital management system (HR system) to populate the employee import records is required. Not all employee records may be sent daily. Some customers choose to manually maintain some data while the primary 305 record may be automated. If multiple employee import files are uploaded to sFTP/in or manually, they all will run during the overnight processing (ONP) window. Remember that all employee import files must start with a 100 record!

RESOURCES:
Import/Extract Administrator User Guide
Upload and Download Files (part of Import/Extract Administrator User Guide)

WEB SERVICES

Concur’s web services offer a powerful suite of user APIs that allow you to create, update, and retrieve users using sophisticated API calls. At the core of these APIs is the User Provisioning v4 API. Many of my peers have also written extensive blogs on the User Provisioning APIs, which are referenced below.

While these APIs replicate, in part, the same processes of manually creating profiles in User Administration or by using the employee import flat files, they are different. It is important to take note of these subtle differences, especially if you are moving from using employee import flat files to using the APIs.

There are six blogs currently posted in the SAP Community on User Provisioning:

  1. Introducing SAP Concur’ s Identity and User Provisioning Webservices | SAP Blogs
  2. Concur User Provisioning Service: Understanding the differences between Identity, Spend and Travel | SAP Blogs
  3. Concur User Provisioning Service: Synchronous vs Asynchronous calls with UPS and how they work together | SAP Blogs
  4. SAP Concur User Provisioning: Assigning Entitlements and Roles | SAP Blogs
  5. Migrating to User Provisioning Service v4 API from User v1 and Bulk v3x | SAP Blogs
  6. Best Practices for Utilizing Concur’s Identity v4 and User Provisioning v4 Webservices | SAP Blogs

ADDITIONAL RESOURCES:
User Provisioning Service v4 – This is a starting point for the UPS API documentation.

I often say there are at least three ways to do something in Concur. When it comes to adding, updating, or removing users, you have four methods depending on your configuration and permissions – User Administration, User Import, flat files, and/or Web Services. Each method has its own purpose, and you may find that a combination of methods is right for your organization. Your implementation team, Concur Support, and the user guides linked as resources above will help you determine the best method for any situation. The user profile provisions what users have access to within their system. It is a critical function within any Concur site. I hope you find this post helpful as you discover what works best for your system.

entry.title: HDS - Historical Data Service
entry.content:
I have been a technical consultant at SAP Concur for two and a half years and have worked with some customers who needed access to historical data after a merger or acquisition and discontinued the use of past databases.

In this post, I will explain how HDS can help our customers to access to the historical data needed to support an internal audit, inquiry or reporting activities.

The Historical Data Service (HDS) is designed for SAP Concur Professional edition customers who are terminating all existing SAP Concur services, including Travel, and wish to maintain access to historical data for audit, compliance, and reporting purposes. This service is particularly beneficial for organizations undergoing mergers, acquisitions, or restructuring, where consolidation of data is necessary.

It allows designated users to view and print essential documents such as receipts, expense reports, invoices, and travel reports, ensuring that businesses can meet their audit and compliance needs even after terminating their Concur services.

Features:

- Restricted access to historical data for up to five approved administrators with Processor Audit and Reporting roles.

- Ability to view and print images, expense reports, invoices, purchase requests, purchase orders, travel reports, and more.

- Ongoing access to the Cognos reporting environment, including custom reports.

- Deactivation of all other users to ensure data security.

 

Required Services and Components (Landscape):

- SAP Concur Professional edition.

- Termination of all existing SAP Concur services, including Travel.

- Cognos reporting environment for continued access to custom reports.

 

Implementation Considerations:

- Designate a key contact within the organization to manage the HDS project.

- Identify and confirm five users who will have read-only access to historical data.

- Ensure all other users are deactivated to maintain data security.

 

Exclusions:

- HDS is not available for Standard edition customers.

- Not suitable for customers planning to keep their travel services active.

- Not applicable for customers who are not fully terminating all existing SAP Concur services.

More info: HDS

entry.title: Revolutionizing Expense Management: SAP Concur App for Microsoft Teams
entry.content:
We are thrilled to announce the launch of the SAP Concur App for Microsoft Teams, now available in the Microsoft App Store. This powerful integration brings expense management directly into the flow of work, providing employees and approvers with seamless collaboration and enhanced productivity. Here’s a closer look at how this integration is transforming expense management workflows.

Key Features of the SAP Concur App for Microsoft Teams:

  1. View, Share, and Approve Expense Reports: Employees can easily view their most recent active expense reports within Microsoft Teams. The list includes key information such as the status of each report. With just one click, employees can access detailed views of their expense reports in SAP Concur, making the process efficient and user-friendly.

    Sharing expense reports with colleagues or approvers has never been easier. Employees can generate a shareable card for any expense report and forward it to a colleague directly within Teams. The recipient, provided they have the necessary permissions and the SAP Concur App enabled, can view the report and take appropriate action.

    Approvers can quickly access their most recent expense reports awaiting approval through the approval queue in Teams. They can review the details, add comments, and approve or send back reports directly within Teams. All comments and actions are tracked in the audit log in SAP Concur, ensuring transparency and compliance.

  2. Streamlined Employee Actions: Employees can view their expense reports and share them without needing to leave Microsoft Teams, allowing them to stay focused and productive within their workflow.

  3. Simplified Approvals: Approvers save time by reviewing and approving expense reports directly in Teams, with no need to switch applications. This integrated process ensures a smooth and efficient workflow.

How It Works:

  • Sign-In and Connect: Once the SAP Concur App is enabled in Teams, users can sign in and connect their Microsoft Teams account with SAP Concur. This ensures that employees can securely retrieve information from SAP Concur.

  • Intuitive Prompts: Users can interact with the app by typing prompts or selecting actions from a list of available prompts. Whether it’s viewing reports, sharing them, or approving them, the app provides clear and simple workflows.

Prerequisites for Using the SAP Concur for Microsoft Teams App:

To use the SAP Concur for Microsoft Teams app seamlessly, your organization must:

  • Have a contract with SAP Concur and have the Concur Travel and/or Concur Expense solution enabled.

  • Be eligible to and opted into the new Concur Expense and its new booking experience.

  • Get an Administrator to activate the app within your Microsoft environment.

For more information on how to get started, check the SAP Concur help portal for Microsoft Teams.

The Value of Integration:

The SAP Concur App for Microsoft Teams eliminates the need to switch between applications, saving time and improving focus. By bringing expense management into the Teams environment, employees and approvers can:

  • Improve efficiency by managing expenses within their existing collaboration tools.

  • Enhance transparency with easy sharing and tracking of expense reports.

  • Boost productivity by reducing friction in the expense approval process.

This integration is a game-changer for organizations looking to modernize their expense management practices and empower employees with tools that adapt to their workflows.

Ready to Get Started?

The SAP Concur App for Microsoft Teams is now available in the Microsoft App Store. Enable it today and experience the future of seamless expense management. Together, let’s make expense management smarter, faster, and more collaborative.

entry.title: Extending Journal Content and Structure with SAP Concur Expense Data
entry.content:
Travel-to-reimburse:

Extending Journal Content and Structure with SAP Concur Expense Data (Use case: Extending the Journal Entry with the vendor description)

In today's fast-paced business environment, integrating and extending financial data across platforms is crucial for maintaining accurate and comprehensive records and extending the capability of SAP S/4HANA to meet unique business needs which can sometimes be challenging.

When SAP Financial systems receive expense reports from SAP Concur, only standard and limited fields are passed to the journal document.

To improve data tracking and for Reporting purposes, some customers need to customize and add additional Expense Data to the Journal document.  The following passages outline a process to make such enhancements possible.

This blog explains how to extend journal content and structure using the mapping of custom fields between both systems: SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud and SAP Concur

 

Use Case 1: Adding vendor description to the Journal Entry Structure.

This blog post outlines one of business requirements on how to enhance the journal content and structure by incorporating Concur Expense data into SAP S/4 HANA public Cloud edition by adding the vendor description in the journal entry to offer several benefits:

  • Simplifies Reconciliation: Provides context for payments and liabilities.
  • Speeds Up Audit Process: Facilitates quicker audits by providing detailed transaction information.
  • Enhances Internal Controls: Reduces fraud risks such as duplicate payments.

 

To extend the financial Journal Entry with vendor description maintained in the expense report, follow these steps:

1. Create a Custom Field (Vendor Description) in S/4 HANA Public Cloud.

Open the “Custom Fields and Logic” App and Create a New Custom Field:

  • Click on "Create".
  • Select the Business Context relevant to where you want the custom field to be (Journal Entry).

Define the Custom Field:

  • Enter a Label and Name for the custom field.
  • Choose the Type of field (e.g., Text, Number, Date).
  • Set the Length and other properties as required.

 

Enable Field Usage:

  • In the UIs and Reports tab, enable the field for the relevant applications and reports where you want it to appear.
  • You can also enable the field for OData Services if needed.

Save and Publish:

  • Click "Save" to save your custom field.
  • Click "Publish" to make the field available in the selected business context.

 For more information about the creation of the custom field in SAP S/4 Hana Public Cloud,

see Editing Custom Fields.

2. Create a Substitution Rule:

use the "Manage Substitution and Validation Rules" app to map the custom field to the vendor description:

Open the Fiori App:

  • Navigate to the Fiori launchpad and open the "Manage Substitution and Validation Rules" app.

Create a New Rule:

  • Click on "Create Rule".
  • Enter the Business Context (Expense Report: Line Item Level).
  • Select the Event (Expense Report: Line Item Level).
  • Choose Substitution Rule as the Rule Type.
  • Click on "Create".

 Define General Information:

  • Enter a Rule Name and Description.

Set Preconditions (if any): Optional

  • Define any preconditions that must be met for the substitution to occur. This can include specific field values or conditions.

Define Substitution Details:

  • Under the Substitution section, specify the Substitute Type (Substitute with field).
  • Enter the Target Field: the field created in step 1.
  • Enter the Source Field: vendor description.

 

Save and Activate:

  • Click on "Save" to save the rule.
  • Click on "Activate" to make the rule active.

 

3. Creating an expense Report in Concur with vendor details:

This is an example of an Expense report with vendors data.

 

 4. Posting the Expense Report:

Import the document manually or waiting for the automatic import, the posting results to the bellow extended Journal entry, adding the vendor description in the structure.  

 

 Note: the vendor description should only be displayed in the Debit Line Item, and if you have the requirement to clear the vendor on the credit line item, an Implementation of custom logic (BAdI) is needed.

             

Conclusion

Integrating Concur Expense data into S/4 HANA's journal entries enhances financial transparency and control. By following the outlined steps, businesses can achieve a more detailed and accurate financial reporting system.

Stay tuned for more blog posts that will showcase additional use cases covering other business requirements needed to extend the structure and the content of the Journal Entry with SAP Concur expense data.

We always welcome feedback for our SAP Concur Integration (ICS) so if you have any topics that you think would make for an interesting and beneficial blog, then please feel free to send your blog suggestions to our team inbox: sapconcurintegartion@sap.com

entry.title: Concur Quick Expense v4 API
entry.content:
I am a Technical Consultant with SAP Concur and work with customers that wish to interface their data with Concur Expense and Concur Invoice. I regularly collaborate with customers that use APIs to manage the data exchange between their systems and the Expense solution provided by SAP Concur.

The Quick Expense API facilitates the creation of an expense entry by capturing essential details, including date, amount, and type of expense, with the option to attach a receipt image. This new entry can then be added to an expense report within Concur Expense, enabling the user to provide further specifics such as attendees and itemizations.

The Quick Expense API can be useful in many scenarios. Transactions can be created to aid in testing cycles, easing the burden on your testers to manually create expense entries. If you have a card program that is not able to be automatically imported into Concur from the card provider, you can automate the import of these transactions from information provided to you by the provider. The time savings from being able to programmatically create these transactions can be a significant savings for your organization.

Prerequisites

This post will not discuss authentication with Concur Expense. Please see this post to get an explanation on authentication and application building with Concur Expense.

You will need an application with the following SCOPEs.

Required Scopes:

  • quickexpense.writeonly          - Write quick expense
  • user.read                                - Get User Information, necessary for userID if using a user token
  • receipts.writeonly                   - Required if e-Bunsho is enabled

 

Optional Scope:

  • CONFIG                                 - Get Expense Configuration info, necessary for expenseTypeId

 

Works with these SAP Concur solutions:

  • Expense – Standard
  • Expense – Professional
  • Japan Market: If the partner is using this API to provide the e-Bunsho digital timestamp for the receipt, the partner should confirm that the client has e-Bunsho activated in SAP Concur

Introduction

The Quick Expense API will be discussed with examples for creating the transactions with and without an image. The images shown are tested using Opensource client API Bruno v.1.34.2. You may need to adjust the payload/configuration based upon the API client used.

Additional details regarding the Quick Expense v4 API can be found here.

Detailed Walkthrough

Determine the userID for the Quick Expense

The first step you should complete is to determine the userID that the transaction will be assigned. You can retrieve the profile of the user using this Profile API. This is the reason the user.read scope is listed as required for the Quick Expense API. This API could be used to determine the userID of the user making the profile API call.

GET     https://{datacenterURI}/profile/v1/me?schema=compact

If you wish to determine the userID for the given user by calling another Concur API, there are many to choose from that can be found in the Developer center documentation. These may require additional scopes to be added to the application. One example would be the use of the Identity v4.0 API.

Once the userID is determined, the expenseTypeId will need to be found.

Determine the expenseTypeId for the Quick Expense

The next step that must be completed is to determine the policies that the user is assigned. Transactions can only be created for expense types assigned to policies the user has been given. So, you must first determine the policies that the user has been assigned. Once you have identified the policies the user has been assigned, then you can determine the expense types available for the transaction which will be created by the Quick Expense API. If the Quick Expense API is not supplied an expense type, or a transaction is assigned an expense type that is not allowed for the user, the API will fail.

Determine the policies that are assigned for the user. The userID will be the value found in the last step.

GET     https://{datacenterURI}/expenseconfig/v4/users/{userID}/policies

Additional details regarding the Expense Configuration v4 APIs can be found here.

Example of the policy results for a given userID:

Next, determine the list of expense types available to a user based on a particular policy found in the previous API call. Based on the results, one of the expenseTypeId values will be used to create the transaction using the Quick Expense API.

GET     https://{datacenterURI}/expenseconfig/v4/users/{userID}/policies/{policyId}/expensetypes

Additional details regarding the Expense Configuration v4 APIs can be found here.

Example of the expense type results for a given userID and policyId:

Creating a Quick Expense without an Image

You have now determined the userID that the transaction will be assigned as well as the expenseTypeId. The template for this API call is listed below. Additional details can be found here.

Template for Quick Expense v4 API without an Image

POST   https://{datacenterURI}/quickexpense/v4/users/{userID}/context/{contextType}/quickexpenses

 

Name

Type

Description

userID

string

Required: The unique identifier of the SAP Concur user.

contextType

string

Required: The access level of the SAP Concur user, which determines the form fields they can view/modify. Supported value: TRAVELER.

You are now ready to create the payload for the Quick Expense.

First, determine the paymentTypeId that should be used for the quick expense.

Supported values:

  • CASHX  – Cash
  • CPAID   – Company Paid
  • PENDC – Pending Card Transaction

Next, create the body of the payload with the appropriate values that you wish to load for the user. I have an example payload below that you can use as a reference.

{
  "comment": "Team Lunch to Discuss Quick Expense",
  "expenseTypeId": "MEALS",
  "location": {
       "city": "Atlanta",
       "countryCode": "US",
       "countrySubDivisionCode": "US-GA"
  },
  "paymentTypeId": "CASHX",
  "transactionAmount": {
       "currencyCode": "USD",
       "value": 30.99
  },
  "transactionDate": "2024-12-27",
  "vendor": "Subway"
}

Then, call the Quick Expense v4 API with your payload.

Example of the results using the above payload:

This is what the expense will appear as in Concur.

There may be additional details required for the Concur user to enter once the expense is assigned to a report which is dependent upon the Concur configuration.

 

Creating a Quick Expense with an Image

As discussed in the previous section, you have now determined the userID that the transaction will be assigned as well as the expenseTypeId.

The template for this API call is listed below. Additional details can be found here.

Template for Quick Expense v4 API with an Image

POST     https://{datacenterURI}/quickexpense/v4/users/{userID}/context/{contextType}/quickexpenses/image

 

Name

Type

Description

userID

string

Required The unique identifier of the SAP Concur user.

contextType

string

Required The access level of the SAP Concur user, which determines the form fields they can view/modify. Supported values: TRAVELER.

fileContent

file

Required The quick expense image. Maximum size 50 MB. Supported image types are: PNG, PDF, TIFF, JPEG

Creating a quick expense with an image requires a multi-part form.

You can use the same format for the body as the previous example. A key value is added for the body titled “quickExpenseRequest”.

{
  "comment": "Team Lunch to Discuss Quick Expense",
  "expenseTypeId": "MEALS",
  "location": {
       "city": "New York",
       "countryCode": "US",
       "countrySubDivisionCode": "US-NY"
  },
  "paymentTypeId": "CASHX",
  "transactionAmount": {
       "currencyCode": "USD",
       "value": 33.87
  },
  "transactionDate": "2024-12-27",
  "vendor": "Good Restaurant"
}

A key value is also added for the body titled “fileContent”. The image that you wish to load should then be specified.

Example of the results using the above quickExpenseRequest payload and a fileContent image:

This is what the expense will appear as in Concur.

Conclusion

The Quick Expense API can easily be used to create expenses that can be assigned to a user. There can be significant time savings gained by using this API whether it is utilized to create test transactions or create transactions that could not be created by a card vendor.

Share and Connect

What do you think?  Do you have anything to add? Leave a comment below.

Did you find it useful? Give us a like and share on social media.

Want to know more about SAP Concur? Please follow here.

Want to ask questions about SAP Concur and its offerings? Ask here

Follow my profile for similar content.

Thank you!

entry.title: Are you looking for administration and configuration tools to manage Concur Request. Concur Request?
entry.content:
I  recommend to access our Getting Started with Concur Request Professional for Administrators learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey is designed to to introduce you to the common administration and configuration tools, to manage Concur Request. Concur Request is a powerful tool that allows users to document and preauthorize upcoming expenses before they occur. This allows for better budgeting and allocation of funds. Concur Request accommodates both travel and non-travel related expenses.

Learning objectives
Upon completion of this learning journey, administrators will be able to administer and configure Concur Request using common tools such as List Management, Audit Rules, Email Reminders, and more. 

Prerequisites

  • Administrators should have a firm understanding of how Concur Request works as a business user.
    Review the learning journey, Getting Started with Concur Professional Request for Business Users, before completing this learning journey.

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 
Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Are you an administrators and want to learn how to work with Invoice Pay in Concur Invoice Standard?
entry.content:
I  recommend to access our Working with Invoice Pay in Concur Invoice Standard learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey, for administrators, focuses on configuring and maintaining payment configurations using Automated Clearing House (ACH), Virtual Cards, or Checks in Concur Invoice Standard with the Invoice Pay service.

Learning objectives
After completing this course, administrators will be able to identify and explain the key features of Concur Invoice Payment Manager including how to configure and maintain ACH, Virtual Cards, and Check payments, to ensure payments are made accurately and on-time to vendors. 

Prerequisites

  • Administrators should have a basic understanding of Concur Invoice and the Procure to Pay process.

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Are you a Business User and want to learn the basics of Concur Request?
entry.content:
I  recommend to access our Getting Started with Concur Standard Request for Business Users learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey is an introduction to Concur Request for Business Users. In this comprehensive journey, you will learn the basics of Concur Request, from creating and working with requests to reviewing and approving them as an approver.

Learning objectives
After completing this course, business users will be able to proficiently manage their requests by using the Concur Request platform to submit, approve, and process requests.

Prerequisites

  • A general understanding of SAP Concur including Concur Expense and Concur Travel.

 Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Are you an administrator and want to getting up and running Concur Invoice Payment Manager?
entry.content:
I  recommend to access our Working with Invoice Pay in Concur Invoice Professional learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey, for administrators, focuses on configuring and maintaining payment configurations using Automated Clearing House (ACH), Virtual Cards, or Checks in Concur Invoice Professional with the Invoice Pay service.

Learning Objectives
After completing this course, administrators will be able to identify and explain the key features of Concur Invoice Payment Manager including how to configure and maintain ACH, Virtual Cards, and Check payments, to ensure payments are made accurately and on-time to vendors. 

Prerequisites

  • Administrators should have a basic understanding of Concur Invoice and the Procure to Pay process.  

 Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Unlocking New Possibilities with the GROW with SAP Premium Package
entry.content:
Last year SAP announced a new packaged solution that bundles various cloud-based solutions:
GROW with SAP. This Blog will focus on the GROW with SAP Premium offering and how it relates to
Spend Management with SAP Concur. 
In today's fast-paced business environment, companies need robust solutions to stay ahead of the curve. With the GROW with SAP Premium package, SAP announced a solution with access to SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Concur Expense. This bundle delivers an embedded integration between SAP Cloud ERP and SAP Concur Expense through SAP ICS. This powerful combination is designed to help organizations streamline their operations, enhance financial visibility, and drive growth. 

What is GROW with SAP? 

GROW with SAP is a comprehensive cloud ERP solution tailored for fast-growing organizations. It consists of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition targeting midmarket businesses by offering a complete suite of solutions, services, and learning resources to help companies successfully adopt cloud ERP.  

What is GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Premium? 

GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, premium enriches SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with features that accelerate development and automation and capabilities for procurement and advanced financial management.  
With the GROW with SAP Premium package, businesses can now access SAP Concur Expense solutions and SAP Sales Cloud, providing a holistic approach to managing their operations. 

What is the new offer? 
The GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, premium Edition with material number 8014761, includes additional entitlements for:  

  • SAP Concur Expense (100 transactions per month)   
  • SAP Sales Cloud (15 users) 

In addition to the above entitlements of GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the premium package also includes many more features to accelerate development and automation for advanced financial management and procurement. 
Find more details here: https://www.sap.com/products/erp/grow/how-to-buy.html   

What is SAP Concur Expense? 

SAP Concur Expense empowers organizations to enforce spend management policies, adapt to changing requirements and regulations, and future-proof their organizations. Use SAP Concur Expense to simplify, automate and manage employee spending from receipt capture to reimbursement. Plus, with the SAP Concur mobile app, employees and supervisors can effectively manage their travel and spending from any location, on any device.  

The GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, premium package includes the following:  

  • Concur Expense including 100 expense reports per month 
  • Analytics Reporting 
  • SAP Integration with Concur between SAP Concur Expense and SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition via SAP ICS. 

How is SAP Concur Expense integrated in the offering? 

The integration of SAP Concur Expense with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is managed through the embedded financial integration module known as SAP ICS. 
This integration ensures that all expense data is fully synchronized, providing organizations with end-to-end visibility of their spending in near real-time. Here are some key benefits: 

  • Enhanced Financial Visibility: With Concur Expense, businesses can manage their employee spend quickly, simply, and accurately. The integration provides complete visibility into business expenses, allowing companies to evaluate trends and identify potential cost savings.  
  • Streamlined Operations through Automation: The seamless data synchronization between Concur Expense and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition ensures that organizations can manage their expenses efficiently. This integration eliminates the need for manual data entry, reducing errors and saving time while providing timely and accurate reimbursements.  
  • Improved Decision-Making: By leveraging the powerful analytics and reporting capabilities of Concur Expense, businesses can gain valuable insights into their spending patterns. This information can be used to make informed decisions and optimize performance.  

How is GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Premium delivered? 

The GROW with SAP Premium package is delivered as a ready-to-run service (SaaS) with pre-configured industry best practices and automated system provisioning. GROW with SAP delivers a ready-to-run SaaS ERP backed by 50 years of industry experience. When combined with Concur Expense, organizations can unlock new possibilities and drive significant growth. 

Conclusion 

The GROW with SAP Premium package, including the seamless integration between SAP Cloud ERP and SAP Concur Expense, is a game-changer for businesses looking to enhance their financial visibility and streamline their operations. By leveraging this powerful combination, organizations can stay ahead of the competition and achieve their growth objectives. 

For more information about the GROW with SAP Premium package and how it can benefit your organization, visit https://www.sap.com/products/erp/grow.html .

entry.title: SAP Integration with Concur Solutions becomes available for Concur Standard
entry.content:
Get ready for the next step in intelligent travel and expense management! Starting with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502, the SAP Integration with Concur Solutions (a.k.a. ICS a.k.a. native integration) supports a seamless integration with SAP Concur Standard Edition. This native integration offers the benefits of a streamlined integration setup by delivering the next level of automated financial integration and real-time data synchronization for growing businesses using SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

For those of you who aren't familiar yet with ICS, let me give you a short overview.

ICS is available for 

  • SAP ECC 6.0 and SAP Concur Professional Edition
  • SAP S/4HANA and SAP Concur Professional Edition
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP Concur Professional Edition
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition with SAP Concur Professional Edition and SAP Concur Standard Edition

While the scope varies for the different ERP and SAP Concur environments, you can integrate such master data like cost objects and transactional data like expense reports and cash advances.

For integrations between SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP Concur Standard Edition, consider the following:

  • Employee replication using ICS is not available. Instead, you can use the SAP Master Data Integration service to handle the replication of your employee master data in your landscape.
  • The Enhanced WBS Element Search feature is not supported.
  • The Budget Integration feature is not yet supported.
  • Processing payroll documents is not yet supported.

If you got questions/feedback, please leave a comment.

entry.title: SAP Integration with Concur Solutions: Replicating Authorized and Cost Object Approvers
entry.content:
With the S/4HANA 2023 release, the user replication of SAP Integration with Concur Solutions, the standard integration between SAP Concur and SAP S/4HANA a.k.a. ICS a.k.a. Native Integration, has been switched to the User Provisioning (UPS) v4 API. That API allows to replicate a lot more user-/employee-specific data than the previously used User v1, which has been deprecated.

Mid of 2024, the UPS API has been enhanced to support the replication of Authorized and Cost Object Approvers, a long awaited feature. With S/4HANA 2023, feature pack 3, ICS will support that feature end-2-end. I will explain how to set up a simple Cost Object Approver replication to explain the new functionality.

 

Use Case

All expenses are allocated to cost centers. The cost center responsible is supposed to approve all expenses which are allocated to their cost center.

 

How To Configure The Cost Center Approver

Prerequisites:

  • In the SAP Concur system the cost object list must be utilized for the cost object approval to make use of this functionality.
  • Maintain the employee who will be the cost center owner as such in field User Responsible (VERAK_USER) for all cost centers. The linkage between the user record and the employee record is stored in infotype 105 (Communication), sub type 0001 (System User Name), field System ID.

If you call up the field mapping of the employee replication within the transaction Concur Integration Setup (CTE_SETUP), you will see the new section spendApproverLimit. Beneath that section, you will see two more sections: authorizedApprover and costObjectApprover.

New sections for spendApproverLimit, costObjectApprover, and authorizedApprover

 

Press the Create button to the right of the costObjectApprover section. A pop-up will be raised where you need to select the approvalType. Select report for defining the costObjectApprover for an expense report.

Select report as approvalType

A data set for defining the mapping for an expense report's costObjectApprover is created. The approvalType is defaulted to report. Implementation code EE_COST_CENTER_OWNER is defaulted for determining the approvalGroup. 

Implementation Code EE_COST_CENTER_OWNER is defaulted

The implementation code EE_COST_CENTER_OWNER works as follows:

  1. Determine all cost centers where the employee who is supposed to be replicated is maintained as User Responsible (VERAK_USER).
  2. Add a data record to assign the employee as cost center approver to each cost center from Step 1. In the payload that is sent to Concur, you will see the approvalGroup field filled according to the definition for the cost center replication.
    Let's assume that you use the set up which is considered as Best Practice, i.e., Logical System -> Company Code -> Cost Object Type -> Cost Object ID. Then the value in the approvalGroup field will be the concatenation of those 4 values separated by a dash, e.g. QVGCLNT354-1710-CC-SE_301024.

Depending on your Concur set up, you might also have to provide values for the other fields in the costObjectApprover section, e.g. approvalLimit, reimbursementCurrency, level ...

 

What's Missing

So far, we've defined that an employee can be replicated as cost center approver for expense reports. But we haven't defined yet that the employee should also get the related authorization (role Expense Approver, EXP_APPROVER).
The role assignment is done via the section spendRole. If you haven't defined a mapping for the Expense Approver role yet, press the Create button for spendRole and select EXP_APPROVER from the pop-up. 

 

Select Expense Approver role

Now, you can define when the Expense Approver role should be assigned to an employee. To assign the role to an employee that is maintained as User Responsible for a cost center (see also the explanation above for EE_COST_CENTER_OWNER),  pick the implementation code EE_IS_CC_RESPONSIBLE for the field roleName.

Enter EE_IS_CC_RESPONSIBLE as implementation code for the field roleName

Save your changes. From now on, the system will replicate an employee as cost object approver if the employee is the User Responsible for at least 1 cost center.

 

Additional Remarks

  • Authorized approvers are configured in a similar manner as cost object approvers.
  • For determining whether an employee should be assigned an approver role, the implementation code EE_IS_RLU_MGR_OR_CC_RESP will also be available. The implementation code combines the functionalities of EE_IS_RLU_MANAGER and  EE_IS_CC_RESPONSIBLE.
  • The BAdI BADI_CTE_HCM_EE_DATA_CHANGE_V4 has been enhanced to cover spendApproverLimit functionality. For example, you can create your own logic to determine the approvalGroup by implementing the BAdI.

entry.title: Are you an administrator and need an introduction to configuration tools of Concur Expense Standard?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Getting Started with Concur Expense Standard for Administrators learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey is an introduction to Concur Expense Standard for Administrators and Functional Consultants. In this comprehensive journey, you will learn the configuration tools and components of Concur Expense Standard, from the Basic and Core Configuration, to Payments, and finally the optional configurations.

Learning objectives
At the end of this learning journey, administrators and functional consultants will be able to,  in Concur Expense Standard:

  • Navigate the Expense Settings page
  • Configure the tools and components
  • Process payments and extracts
  • Navigate reporting 

Prerequisites
Learners should have a firm understanding of how Concur Expense operates as a business user.
Please complete the Getting Started with Concur Expense for Customer Users learning journey before completing this course. 

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit
